note: Keyed, open-growing taxonomy for the design-reference corpus (#382). Ratified by the #394 ruling (forks B/C/B/A). Two orthogonal author-supplied axes: `productRegister` (the product/audience type the app serves) and `category` (the coarse functional domain). A third axis, `visualStyle`, is NOT seeded here — it is filled by the phase-3 vision pass (#475/#396), left null at collect time. The pipeline (`report`, `collect`, `harvest`) validates target/sidecar values against these lists and WARNS (never errors) on an unregistered value, so the vocab can grow without blocking a run. Add values freely; keep them coarse.

productRegister.description: The product / audience register an app targets — the WHO, not the look. Orthogonal to visualStyle (the vision-filled look-and-feel).

values.label: Enterprise
values.note: B2B/admin/ops tooling; dense, role-driven, governance-aware (Grafana, Swagger).

values.label: Modern SaaS
values.note: Polished product-led web apps; marketing-grade UI, freemium (CodePen, Mermaid, Tailwind Play).

values.label: Creative tool
values.note: Canvas/maker apps for design/diagram/media work (Excalidraw, tldraw, Photopea, draw.io).

values.label: Utilitarian
values.note: Function-first, unembellished single-purpose tools (regex101, Squoosh, OpenStreetMap, JSFiddle).

category.description: Coarse functional domain (~10+). Fork 4 = A: deliberately coarse, cross-referenced to G2/Capterra top-level names where they map. `at minimum` the first ten; extra coarse domains may be added to cover the corpus.
